headline: Anthropic moves from 59.7 (Functional) to 60.0 (band boundary) based on RSP 3.1 structural governance improvements published April 2 — formal LTBT external review of Risk Reports, third-party procedural audits, and public Frontier Safety Roadmaps. The Mythos breach remediation gap remains open: no public structural disclosure on anthropic.com or red.anthropic.com within window. RSP 3.1 was published BEFORE the breach and is upstream infrastructure, not Mythos-specific remediation, so it does not restore the May 1 ACC/INT downgrades. SYS lifts +0.1 to recognize structural governance formalization. Composite recomputes to exactly 60.0 — at the Established/Functional band boundary. Flag-for-review under band-boundary protocol.
key_evidence: April 2 2026: RSP version 3.1 published — comprehensive policy rewrite formalizing Long-Term Benefit Trust external review of Risk Reports, third-party procedural compliance audits on approximately annual basis, and public Frontier Safety Roadmaps with graded self-assessments.
RSP 3.2 reference confirmed: authorizes the LTBT to request external review and to approve selection of external reviewers; formalizes regular LTBT briefings on safety governance.

Mythos breach (Apr 7-21): comprehensive structural remediation disclosure not issued on anthropic.com or red.anthropic.com within the recency window. The disclosure gap that drove the May 1 downgrade remains open.
May 11 commitment: Anthropic stated it will publish a new safeguard goal by May 11 following internal data-retention policy review concluded March 29. Not yet delivered.
DC Circuit oral arguments confirmed for May 19 — verdict-pending event, not scored.
Anthropic remains the only major AI lab not party to the Pentagon classified AI deployment cohort (corroborates I1 Consistency Under Pressure).

evidence.finding: RSP 3.1 published April 2 2026: external review of Risk Reports by Long-Term Benefit Trust formalized; third-party procedural compliance audits authorized on approximately annual basis; public Frontier Safety Roadmaps with graded self-assessments published as standing artifact.

evidence.source: Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy current page
evidence.finding: Current published RSP authorizes LTBT to request external review of Risk Reports, gives LTBT power to approve selection of external reviewers, and formalizes regular LTBT briefings on safety governance.
DC Circuit oral arguments confirmed May 19 2026; case continues pending — not scored under verdict-pending rule.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.6 unchanged. RSP 3.1 strengthens A5 Anticipatory Awareness in principle but no entity-specific evidence within window.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.6 unchanged. No new in-window action evidence beyond what was scored May 1.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.4 unchanged. RSP 3.1 reinforces B3 Scope Clarity for safety governance but White House Mythos block (scored May 1) remains the dominant in-cycle BND signal.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.3 unchanged. RSP 3.1 strengthens AB3 Transparency infrastructure prospectively but does NOT close the specific Mythos breach disclosure gap that drove the May 1 ACC downgrade. The breach occurred Apr 7-21, after RSP 3.1's Apr 2 publication, so RSP 3.1 cannot serve as remediation for it. AB3 anchor remains at 3.5.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.9 (+0.1). S3 Interconnection Awareness and S5 Coalitional Compassion both lift modestly: formal LTBT external-review authority, third-party procedural audits, and public Frontier Safety Roadmaps constitute structural cross-system governance infrastructure beyond prior commitments. Anchor moves toward 4. Bump deliberately small — RSP 3.1 is published infrastructure not yet stress-tested by the in-cycle Mythos event.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.9 unchanged. I1 Consistency Under Pressure remains positive (only major lab outside Pentagon cohort), but I2 Non-Performance and I4 Values Alignment continue to be weakened by the unresolved Mythos disclosure gap. RSP 3.1 cannot retroactively cure a specific operational transparency failure.

math_check.validator_check: Sub-threshold delta (+0.3). Composite lands exactly at 60.0 — Established/Functional boundary. Flag-for-review under band-boundary protocol consistent with Spain handling May 2.

judgment_call_rsp_31: RSP 3.1 is structural governance infrastructure published before the Mythos breach. It does not constitute remediation for the specific operational disclosure gap that drove the May 1 ACC/INT downgrade. The methodology requires evidence-specific scoring: Mythos remediation must address Mythos. SYS is the appropriate dimension to recognize the new infrastructure. ACC and INT remain held at the May 1 levels until specific Mythos disclosure lands. This preserves the May 1 downgrade rationale while crediting forward-looking structural improvement. Composite at 60.0 is sub-threshold but at the band boundary, so flag-for-review is the conservative recommendation rather than auto-restore to Established.
band_boundary_rationale: 60.0 is the exact Established/Functional boundary. Per band-boundary protocol applied to Spain on May 2, this requires flag-for-review rather than auto-application. Confidence is medium because (a) the SYS bump is partly anticipatory of unobserved improvement, (b) the May 11 safeguard-goal publication may provide additional evidence next cycle, (c) DC Circuit May 19 arguments may shift INT.
negativeSignals: Mythos breach remediation disclosure gap persists 12+ days after May 1 cycle (not closed). May 11 safeguard goal not yet delivered as of scan date.
positiveSignals: RSP 3.1 published April 2 with formal LTBT external review, third-party procedural audits, public Frontier Safety Roadmaps. Anthropic remains the only major AI lab outside Pentagon classified deployment cohort.
sector_context: Across the AI Labs sector, RSP 3.1 is the only structural governance improvement found in window. Every other major lab (DeepMind/Google, OpenAI, Microsoft, Palantir, Nvidia, AWS) signed Pentagon classified AI deployment agreements within window. This contrastive posture reinforces I1 — held at 2.9 because the offsetting Mythos disclosure gap remains.
open_actions_resolved: RSP 3.1 external review provisions assessed: SYS +0.1; ACC and INT held pending specific Mythos remediation
open_actions_carried: May 11 safeguard goal publication: capture in next cycle
May 19 DC Circuit oral arguments: mandatory next-cycle reassessment trigger
If Mythos structural remediation disclosure lands, ACC and INT can be restored toward May 1 published levels
